presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The documents address distinct control domains—evidence management for vendor oversight versus privileged access governance—with no overlapping operational scope or shared requirements.

- EV-002, EV-003, and EV-005 require resolution to close the Q1 2024 vendor-control review.  
- Access is restricted to ticketed support and break-fix tasks only.  
- Vendor confirmation of personnel is required quarterly.  
- Evidence repository paths and status designations are critical for tracking provenance.  
- Standing privileged access is approved conditionally for two administrators.  
- Control closure depends on completing outstanding evidence items.
key_highlights: The documents address distinct control domains—evidence management for vendor oversight versus privileged access governance—with no overlapping operational scope or shared requirements.
EV-002, EV-003, and EV-005 require resolution to close the Q1 2024 vendor-control review.
Access is restricted to ticketed support and break-fix tasks only.
Vendor confirmation of personnel is required quarterly.
Evidence repository paths and status designations are critical for tracking provenance.
Standing privileged access is approved conditionally for two administrators.
